payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's late afternoon, I believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Clearly, we're going to talk to El Jefe. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Got it out of the way right away. Autocorrect. Inside story. Inside joke. Whatever.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The inside story.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I think we should go talk to him. That seems like it would be smart, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, and we already signed one contract with him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's not like he wouldn't know where we went if we left, anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What time of day is it right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If I recall, it's later afternoon.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That is my voice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm dialing it in still. (vocalizes trying to find Nott's voice)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And we got there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A little more mid, a little less treble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You make your way across the Pentamarket, to the familiar down, dank, and broody interior of the Evening Nip tavern. You pass by Clive, who gives you a nod. You head towards the hidden staircase down to the subterranean tavern. There a different sound catches your ears. There's the familiar sound of warm music and tavern flair, but you also hear rowdy shouts and cheers and the occasional sound of something heavy against a skidding table. As you slowly descend toward the base of the curved staircase to enter the main chamber, you can see some folks are sitting at the tables, The Gentleman is in his far corner at the back, and they're all watching a small circle of people in the center as two individuals in the process of beating the shit out of each other. Bare fists, bare knuckled. People are making bets, laughing and cheering, and throwing beer and various objects into the ring to mess with them. It is mid-craze scuffle at the moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And we got there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A little more mid, a little less treble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's late afternoon, I believe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can head over.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I get nervous spending too much time in one place, but if everyone's going, then we're going.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's like a step in the way out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if what he wants us to do means that we have to stay here for a while, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Then we'll have to make it worth our while.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like for a lot of money, would you stay?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're at a table, you're doing well. Eventually, you're going to want to walk away from the table.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if the table offers you four times the amount you've ever seen?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The table always offers you four times the amount you've ever seen. You got to walk away while you're winning. But all right!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we'll make a decision.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Getting a glimpse here as you watch a bit of parting between shoulders as everyone's standing end to end. You hear this heavy crack and the two people nearest to you bow backward. Apparently, one of the two fighting individuals being thrusted into the circle is immediately pushed back into the center. You hear another couple of swings go before they both breakaway. You can hear the heavy breaths of them now exhausted, pouring with sweat. You recognize them, one is Louis, one of the agents who fled from the research facility that you were then going after, and the other is Thed, the halfling fellow with the neck tattoo that you saved in the sewers not but a week and half earlier. They are both bloodied--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's a fight club! You guys, it's a fucking fight club!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Beau is in her element.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys step down into the main area, the large ogre bodyguard stands and turns back. His low guttural grunt come through his protruding jawline with a (grunt) and gives you a sign to continue inward.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How have you been, Kutha?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, me too, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Louis is desperately trying to pull the boot of Thed up off his throat, but Thed's able to keep it down. He's pushing and pushing. He spits blood right in the face of Louis, like onto one of his eyes. The consciousness of Louis begins to fade, eyes roll back, and then eventually, as the rest of his body goes limp, they pull Thed off. "Yeah!" They cheer and lift him up and carry him off. They pull Louis up into a chair and start slapping him and pour water in his face. He comes to consciousness and spits out a tooth, and then the crowd's energy begins to die down a bit, and they drag them to different sides of the room and the people being to go about their business, get another drink at the bar.

preceding_turns.text: That was amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The crowd begins to go back to their scenarios. Some of them seem confused by the display of joyous hugging afterwards. Some seem to be down with it and are like, "Yeah, this is what it is about!"

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "A sovereign town, outside of the Dwendalian Empire and outside of the law. That uses the Savalierwood to keep the Crown at bay. Spoken of as a haven for criminals, thieves, murderers, but really, it's home to the truly free entrepreneurs of Wildemount... and criminals, thieves, and murderers. Now, Ophelia is unable to enact on any partnerships at the moment, as her current business is tangled up in some of the local riffraff and she's requested aid in stabilizing her situation. What I propose, as you've already proven yourselves effective problem solvers, self-starters with enough sense of honor to see a deal through, is for you all to travel northward, past the Silberquel Ridge to Shadycreek Run to parlay with Ophelia. Help her in absolving her from her current mire of complications and return with her here to Zadash. For this task completed, I offer an advance of 500 gold pieces and a purse of 4,500 gold pieces upon returning with Miss Mardun within the month."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He means Louis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anyway, if you have any interests--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking Louis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Indeed." You glance back over your shoulder and see Louis at the bar, who's still dazed and "drinking a giant cup of ale and wiping the blood off of his whole torso. "If you've interest in "making coin, doing some interesting work and getting to see most of our beautiful, imperial countryside, I can go on."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja, who are these friends of yours up north?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: Yes, we have, actually.

payload.raw_text: "A sovereign town, outside of the Dwendalian Empire and outside of the law. That uses the Savalierwood to keep the Crown at bay. Spoken of as a haven for criminals, thieves, murderers, but really, it's home to the truly free entrepreneurs of Wildemount... and criminals, thieves, and murderers. Now, Ophelia is unable to enact on any partnerships at the moment, as her current business is tangled up in some of the local riffraff and she's requested aid in stabilizing her situation. What I propose, as you've already proven yourselves effective problem solvers, self-starters with enough sense of honor to see a deal through, is for you all to travel northward, past the Silberquel Ridge to Shadycreek Run to parlay with Ophelia. Help her in absolving her from her current mire of complications and return with her here to Zadash. For this task completed, I offer an advance of 500 gold pieces and a purse of 4,500 gold pieces upon returning with Miss Mardun within the month."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He means Louis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anyway, if you have any interests--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking Louis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Indeed." You glance back over your shoulder and see Louis at the bar, who's still dazed and "drinking a giant cup of ale and wiping the blood off of his whole torso. "If you've interest in "making coin, doing some interesting work and getting to see most of our beautiful, imperial countryside, I can go on."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja, who are these friends of yours up north?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "An old friend of mine, named Ophelia Mardun. She has a base of operations about 350 miles northeast of here. Past Nogvurot and the Quannah Breach, on the outskirts of Shadycreek Run. I'm sure you've heard of it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we have, actually.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "And the like, I'm sure of it. My contacts in town are refusing to investigate, recounting howls from the deep swamp. I would need you to head up to Berleben. Locate a fellow named Febron Keyes, usually hangs around the Keystone Pub in the Puddles. The Puddles is the part of the town that is, heh, sinking. He's my point man for shipments, and the only one who knows where our safe house in the swamp is that hasn't gone fucking missing. Meet with him, secure the safe house and its contents, and return to me. Consider a 250 gold piece advance and a purse of 1,750 gold pieces upon completion. Heck, if you can handle both (laughs) and with timely mind, there might be a bonus."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Understood. That underground tunnel, that you've already visited, happens to be a major artery for our smuggling business, leading straight up to the Lebenda Swamp. The Lebenda Swamp is a useful place to lose things or people, until you need to find them. As well as a direct route to my contacts in Berleben. We've been rather worried that no shipments have made their way south by the river in weeks. After sending your friend up to Berleben last week, it was discovered that our safe house and storage facility in the swamp has gone dark. One of my men, as well as a handful of theirs in Berleben, went to investigate and never returned. Now the swamp-folk are a superstitious lot, an unfortunate facet with working with them."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like swap creatures?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Leading to Berleben.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could you describe your friend, Vabron?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Febron Keyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Febron Keyes, (chuckles) he's memorable. Based by his fairly emanate lack of teeth. His one wandering left eye. A bit of a skin condition."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No grill, busted eye. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I just drooled a little. Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "But he's good at the work he does."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What work is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Keeping his mouth shut and knowing where the secrets are and not giving those secrets up. He's been pressed for it a few times and has proven to be trustworthy, and I admire that."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Would we have the same sort of timetable for this mission, a month?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The sooner the better, as the longer these shipments continues to elude us, the more money we're losing. Like I said, a timely manner, there may be a bonus."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Anything else?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You don't get an exhaustion point, you just become stunned for a round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hard to keep track of parties outside of our purview.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am over Zadash.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just over it, huh? Plus, it'll give us time to leave? Maybe things will change by the time we come back? Or maybe not. We'll see. Never know what happens outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we're going to take them all? Hop on both of his offers, I think?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, quite frankly, I am a little more comfortable in Quannah Breach anyway.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're more nomadic?

preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: Let's do it!

preceding_turns.text: Let's do it, yeah. We'll pound our drinks; keep them away from Beau because she'll drink all of them.

preceding_turns.text: I just want to make sure we're here for a full hour, though. I'm going to drink slowly.

preceding_turns.text: I can use a little-- yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to sip it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The exhaustion point lasts until you take a long rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: For a round, it's gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: For the Haste?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You're fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just a pain in the ass in combat, not in out of combat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Once it wears off you're like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I thought you get a point of exhaustion after Haste.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Dweez comes up from behind a moment later with a stack of regular parchment and ink. He hands it over. It does not look like it would be the necessary enchanted or prepared inks that you could use to scribe spells per se, but it's parchment and ink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, I would not ensure such a mission to him." He glances past you, in the far corner you can see him now leaning over the bar, two full cups in, starting to get a little dazed on the stool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Louis! Fuck you!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: His hand does this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yep, he's a winner.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You will get 107 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anything else you require?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. This has really whet our appetite.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Swamp pun?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I just can't stop staring at his face, it's soaking wet! Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anyway, if our business is complete. I appreciate your patronage, I look forward to our continued partnership, and I wish you all the luck."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't sweat the small stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fucking shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb hangs back all by himself and says (stuttering) Paper and ink?

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the exterior
payload.raw_text: As you guys charge over to the Pentamarket, you make your way to the exterior of The Invulnerable Vagrant. Stepping inside, there is a rather chill, customer-less interior, as two of the regular Pumats sit at the front. As you step into the chamber, "Oh you're back so soon. I figured you had completed all your intended means of purchasing."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: But you can finally kill him if you want to. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Ah, shit there are four more!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Massacre in downtown Zadash this afternoon.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: As you run off I say  Meet us at the cart and horses.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right. Super fast.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the northern
payload.raw_text: I enjoy it, and I'm sure every Dungeon Master out there who watches knows exactly how I feel when it happens. You gather your things up into the cart, the horses begin to push off and you make your way through the city streets of Zadash to the Northern Wall. You can still see some of the townsfolk coming out and going about their day, but the general air of tension and nervousness still holds court throughout the city's streets. There are clusters of crownsguard. They're escorting the Righteous Brand to the eastern side. You make your way to the northern side of the city where the Signet Wall district begins where you have the large fortresses, where the military stays, and the barracks that surround that region. To the large gate that exits the building out into the rest of the Marrow Valley. As you make it through the large, raised portcullis, along with a few other folks that are in the process of making their own journey into the north or to the west or to the east, you step out onto the dirt road, into the green blowing fields, the cold breeze that's blowing beneath the graying skies of Wildemount and you push off. Being here for a while, it seems strange, but also welcoming to be back on the open road for a moment. You glance around and take a deep breath as that clean post-rain air still seems to linger in the vicinity. As you make a sweep on the horizon, with the rolling hills and patches of trees, catch your vision off to the far east on the outside of the Signet Wall district, you see hundreds if not thousands of soldiers, all gathered and making their way east in small troops. Here the mainstay source, the bulk of military power in the center of the Empire makes its way to war. You see horses, siege engines, and large ballista on wheels all being carried and prepared for the journey to the borders of Xhorhas and beyond. With that, you pick up and continue northward, making your way long the Amber Road, towards to where the Bromkiln Byway carves off to your destination. First, I want to ask you guys: what pace are you travelling at? You can go slow pace if you try to be super stealthy. Normal pace is fine. Fast pace you go quickly, but you threaten exhaustion if you continue to push that way.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: To the Swamp!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: To the Bronchial Byway.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: The Bromkiln, yeah. The Bronchial Byway, the life breath of the land.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: We ruin everything Matt creates.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Sometimes you do make them bulletproof and we get mad. But most of the time, we can crack the code.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You're looking at about a 75-mile journey. With the cart and the horses, you're looking at about 30 miles a day at a normal pace. You're probably looking at two nights' stay on the road before you reach your destination.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think we'll go at a regular pace until we get to the Bromkiln Byway. At the Amber Road, we should be all right.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys push forward and watch as, behind you, the large, looming walls and towers and buildings of Zadash that had been your home for weeks disappear slowly behind you. Once again, you're embraced by the wilds and open landscapes of the valley. You take a moment to reflect upon the insanity and craziness of the urban atmosphere and the peace, as well as the unknown, that comes with traversing such an open plain. I'd say you have maybe a few hours before the sun sets. Right as you hit the intersection between the Amber Road and the Bromkiln Byway, the sun has set. The sky is dark; the clouds prevent starlight from coming through, and what moonlight would be visible is choked, giving you only the faint glimmer of the moon's location behind the cloud cover. What would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think we'll go at a regular pace until we get to the Bromkiln Byway. At the Amber Road, we should be all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're looking at about a 75-mile journey. With the cart and the horses, you're looking at about 30 miles a day at a normal pace. You're probably looking at two nights' stay on the road before you reach your destination.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As the night has fallen and you spend the better part of the next hour trying to look for any physical representation of safety or cover, it is just open rolling hills and field. There might be places where you could stay low and perhaps keep relatively safe, but nothing that sticks out to you, unfortunately. What do you plan to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's nighttime now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I bet someone's going to try to jump out at us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We should not let them do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They do that to people that are on the open road.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why would you tell me that? Now I'm very scared!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are there any tree clusters or rock clusters or boulders or anything that provides shelter?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At your current location? Make a perception check with disadvantage because it is nighttime.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm the wrong person to be doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was going to say I was hoping to find the same thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I put on the goggles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you can both make individual rolls, or one of you can aid the other.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll aid you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take your advantage on your roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nope. That was a two with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What did you roll, a one and a two?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A two and a one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's really shit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys get part way through your watch, conversing, muttering about the recent events, the sun comes up, uneventful.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if you're going to be that impressed by it, but maybe you'll like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You can see your breath (exhales).

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Well, I wonder, I mean, I wonder if anyone else-- I think, is our time up, or--?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think so. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That was such church youth trip flirting!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great first date.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With that, your watch comes uneventful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: God's really cool, right? Right, right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Third and final watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me and Caleb. Me and Molly, I guess. I'm on third watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take third watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nott and Molly. Who's helming this one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What's your-- your perception's probably high as balls.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You don't know!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Advantage on yours. Actually, it would have been the first one. I started to realize, it's advantage, but disadvantage because it's night.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, ten. Why is it disadvantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Because it's night.

payload.raw_text: (as Luigi) Let's-a go. (as Matt) With that, morning comes. Without the necessary heat, the fire, the torch, goes out, and right as the frost begins to cause you to shake yourself awake, the morning comes, melting the dew, and you are prepped up on your cart and ready for the next length of your journey.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Although that would be what I would imagine Jester saying.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: That was just an impression.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: (as Jester) Guys, it's so crazy, you know? (as Taliesin) That was terrible.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Wait, she's like Luigi? (as Luigi) It's a-crazy!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Wild and crazy guys!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Continuing onward, about ten minutes later you get just around the edge of where that hill was, and you can see the source of the smoke. There, nestled between a small cluster of trees and a bit of natural rock formations, a small shack that the smoke is coming from.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is it into the woods, or further along?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
